summary: Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed StartupDelayInMSec=0 under Explorer\Serialize and restored the baseline.

setting.name: Startup Program Delay
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can stagger startup apps instead of launching them all immediately. People disable the delay when they want login to feel quicker, but that can also make startup load spike harder.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want startup apps to launch sooner after sign-in.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Removing the startup delay can make apps appear sooner, but it can also increase boot-time contention and make sign-in feel heavier on slower systems. The current registry mapping is still only repo-observed in this dataset.
setting.professional_notes: The repo documentation and app both use HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Serialize\StartupDelayInMSec = 0 to disable the delay. This research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft document that publishes the key and its exact semantics, so the record remains a low-confidence observed mapping only.

allowed_values.label: Primary Microsoft mapping not captured
allowed_values.meaning: This research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source for the exact StartupDelayInMSec registry semantics.

allowed_values.label: Observed app no-delay profile
allowed_values.meaning: The current app treats 0 as the remove-startup-delay profile, based on repo observation rather than a captured primary Microsoft source.

app_current_implementation.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the current app write and restore cycle for StartupDelayInMSec.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems where this dataset has not yet validated StartupDelayInMSec from a primary Microsoft source

states.rationale: Do not publish a validated startup-delay baseline until the exact registry mapping is backed by a primary Microsoft source.

windows_defaults.notes: This keeps the startup-behavior tweak documented without overstating registry confidence.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Do not publish or apply the registry mapping until a primary Microsoft source is captured.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to document startup delay behavior without overstating registry confidence.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a primary-source Microsoft-backed startup delay control.
tradeoffs: The feature area is documented honestly.
The registry mapping stays blocked from validated publication.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed app write used as the current no-delay startup profile.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting what the app currently writes for startup delay.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a validated Microsoft registry mapping.
tradeoffs: The current app behavior is captured.
The exact registry semantics remain based on repo provenance rather than a captured primary source.
recommended_profiles.notes: Blocked until a primary Microsoft source for StartupDelayInMSec is captured.

evidence.title: Repo system research notes for startup delay
evidence.summary: The repo documents StartupDelayInMSec under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Serialize and notes that setting it to 0 removes the startup delay.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes StartupDelayInMSec = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Ghidra headless search on explorer.exe for Serialize
evidence.summary: Headless Ghidra analysis of explorer.exe with SearchMemoryForStringsRegExArg.java 'Serialize' produced three matches, confirming the Explorer Serialize string path in the binary.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe - Explorer startup delay
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe toggled StartupDelayInMSec to 0 and restored the missing baseline under Explorer\Serialize, confirming the current app write and restore cycle.

decision.why: Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed StartupDelayInMSec transitions through missing -> 0 -> missing on the documented Explorer Serialize path.

open_questions: Can a primary Microsoft source for StartupDelayInMSec be captured?
Should this setting be treated only as an advanced Explorer preference?

validation_proof.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean; see the batch probe output in H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\vm-batch-probe-20260320.json. Ghidra headless analysis of explorer.exe also found three matches for Serialize, which supports the Explorer Serialize path used by the record.
